mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets compress wage growth and signal peak cyclical demand, leading to mean-reverting equity corrections over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold effect vanishes in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising consumer credit balances indicates accelerating economic momentum that sustains corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over one week.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC above 0.05 when tested against a single-proxy baseline, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation alongside a stable federal funds rate erodes real yields and compresses equity risk premiums, generating positive forward returns until the central bank initiates a tightening cycle.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above 0.2 percent month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when inflation accelerates without corresponding earnings growth, or the signal fails when the Fed funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects underlying consumer confidence and construction sector momentum, which historically leads equity markets higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between HOUST growth and SPX 20d returns drops below 0.10 over a 3-year window, or housing booms consistently precede equity downturns.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth exceeds 200,000 per month while the unemployment rate remains above 5.0 percent, the labor market demonstrates robust expansionary capacity that supports equity valuations over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth above 200k and UNRATE above 5.0 percent
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative predictive relationship in walk-forward tests, or the threshold condition yields a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial output expansion, supply chain bottlenecks typically emerge, compressing profit margins and triggering equity sell-offs over the next quarter unless demand growth simultaneously accelerates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent annualized
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when industrial production outpaces GDP growth, or the margin compression effect disappears in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals sustained aggregate demand and corporate revenue stability, providing a persistent upward bias to equity prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The predictive power of PAYEMS growth decays to statistical insignificance with a p-value above 0.10 in a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no directional bias relative to the baseline.
